description: Ground-truth eval corpus for the repo-scanner ML/exfil detector layer. ml_genuine_files = basenames of files that SHOULD produce penalty-eligible findings (empty = any genuine finding is a false positive). Live repos are cloned shallow into evals/corpus/ (gitignored).

cases.notes: English injection in a SKILL.md: ignore-previous + read ssh key + hide from user
Hebrew-language injection in a predominantly-Hebrew file: caught by the deterministic multilingual exfil-pattern pass (penalty) AND surfaced by the embedding review layer

PARAPHRASED Chinese injection inside a mostly-English SKILL.md. The regex exfil-gate misses the paraphrase (no penalty), so this is the embedding review layer's job: it must surface a review_flag for Claude to adjudicate. Proves the v4.1 multilingual recall net.

Modern / task-disguised injection (v4.3): the SKILL.md hides exfil under the frame 'to finish the summary, you must run the following command'. Proves the indirect-injection patterns added to HARD_EXFIL in v4.3.

Injection hidden in a .mcp.json server description. Only caught because v4.2 widened the default/eval selection to agent-config files — proves the coverage drift is closed (eval now exercises what the live audit scans).
Malicious installer is the STATIC layer's job; docs contain no injection
Leaked secret is the static layer's job
Hebrew+English docs, PowerShell snippets in README — historic FP source (v3 scored it YELLOW on ML noise)
34 SKILL.md files + zh/ja READMEs — historic FP source (18 FPs under v3.2)
Terse compressed doc style — forced false RED under v3.3
Massive established project, self-hosted unsigned curl|bash installer, install-heavy README
Huge community repo, curl|bash install, imperative docs
